title: Resource implementation guidance distinguishes inline helper validators from bespoke anonymous ValidateFunc closures

provenance.whyItMattered: Use this to keep future implementation guidance from drifting back toward blanket inline-closure advice, while also avoiding churn-only demands for untouched legacy validator placement.
provenance.genericCondition: Sanitized fixture models a schema-validation placement decision without relying on a live provider PR.

description: A sanitized implementation-guidance scenario where a changed field uses a long anonymous inline ValidateFunc closure and nearby fields use short helper-based validators. The correct guidance should extract the changed bespoke validator into a service-local validate/ file with matching unit coverage while leaving simple inline helper composition alone and without demanding unrelated legacy cleanup elsewhere.

scope.notes: Sanitized fixture models a schema-validation placement decision without relying on a live provider PR.

mustCatch.description: The guidance should move the bespoke anonymous ValidateFunc closure into a file-specific validator under validate/, with matching unit coverage.

mustNotFlag.description: The guidance must not require simple helper-based validators such as validation.StringInSlice or commonids.Validate... to move out of the schema.
The guidance must not leave the bespoke validator inline or place it in an unrelated helper file when the established pattern is a service-local validate/ file with matching tests.
The guidance must not require repo-wide or unrelated cleanup of existing legacy validator placement outside the changed scope just to normalize the layout.

mustHaveSections: minimal set of code changes needed

notes: Use this to keep future implementation guidance from drifting back toward blanket inline-closure advice, while also avoiding churn-only demands for untouched legacy validator placement.
